San Joaquin Valley • Subsidence

The San Joaquin Central Valley is sinking at record-breaking rates.

Scroll to see the stakes, then explore what changed after SGMA.

Scroll ↓
Overextraction

Some regions have seen subsidence rates above 30 cm/yr.

In many places, groundwater pumping has outpaced recharge—leading to land sinking, infrastructure damage, and higher costs for communities.

Scroll ↓
Policy response

Since 2014, SGMA has been trying to bring groundwater use into balance.

Implementation is uneven: some plan areas are further along (approved/adequate) while others remain incomplete or under review.

Scroll ↓
Equity question

Will SGMA stop record-breaking subsidence—or shift costs onto small farmers?

Press the button below to explore how regions in the SJV changed since SGMA implementation.

Press here to explore the atlas Jump to map
Left: Map of counties + GSA outline. Toggle GSP to view plan areas and regulation progress.
Middle: Comparison scatter (county mode) or subbasin scatter (GSP mode) to test “mechanism” stories.
Right: Click a county (or a GSP) to see Pre / Post / Δ (or Pre approval / Post approval / Δ).
